name: Poulet a La Montrachet (Chicken & Mushrooms in Cream Sauc
recipeIngredient: 1 Long stem tarragon or 1 sprig each thyme, rosemary, parsley
recipeInstructions:
1. Wipe the chicken inside and out with a cloth wrung out in boiling water.
2. Cut into 10 serving pieces: 2 drumsticks, 2 thighs, 2 wings, and 2 breasts halved.
3. Scrape the carrots and peel the onions. Wipe the mushrooms with a damp cloth and trim the stalks.
4. Melt the butter in a heavy iron cocotte (heavy frying pan or Dutch oven) over low heat.
5. Add the chicken pieces, carrots, and onions, and herbs tied together. Season with salt and pepper.
6. Cook for 30 minutes, moving ingredients occasionally to prevent coloring.
7. Remove carrots and onions with a slotted spoon, cover, and keep hot over a pan of boiling water.
8. Slice mushrooms finely, add to the pan, sprinkle with salt, and simmer for an additional 20 minutes.
9. Beat egg yolks into cream and set aside.
10. Remove chicken to a heated serving dish, place vegetables in small groups around it, cover, and keep hot.
11. Using the back of a fork, work meat residue from the pan bottom into cooking juices.
12. Bring to boiling point, remove herbs, and draw the pan from heat.
13. Add a tablespoon of pan juices to cream, stir well, and return mixture to the pan.
14. Stir constantly until thick, then pour immediately over chicken and serve without delay.
15. Best served with plain noodles or boiled rice.
Makes 6 servings.
